facts.text: History Congress authorized the first Barnegat Lighthouse back in 1835, and it was completed in 1837.
Barnegat Lighthouse stands 172 feet tall and serves as a vital navigational aid for vessels along the Atlantic Ocean.
A severe winter storm in 1920 eroded shoreline up to the base of the lighthouse, prompting the abandonment of the keeper's house.
Tourism and fishing remain the borough's most prominent industries, echoing its historic reliance on the sea.
Its distinctive red and white coloration makes it a recognizable landmark, and it continues to operate as an active aid to navigation under the U.S. Coast Guard.
Originally constructed in 1859, the lighthouse has witnessed over a century and a half of maritime history, enduring storms, shipwrecks, and the evolution of coastal navigation.
